mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate reduces labor market slack, boosting consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in declining UNRATE periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rate crossing above 4.5%, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in consumer credit balances indicates a supply-demand mismatch that erodes profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in at least 50% of historical instances where INDPRO falls and RSAFS rises concurrently over a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, persistent wage growth accelerates input costs without corresponding productivity gains, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when PAYEMS growth exceeds 0.3% and CPIAUCSL accelerates simultaneously, invalidating the cost-push earnings compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real disposable income and robust retail sales data signal expanding consumer purchasing power, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces a negative or zero Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns in rising RSAFS periods do not statistically differ from flat periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 100, it signals a severe contraction in manufacturing output that reduces corporate earnings guidance, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where INDPRO drops below 100, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate combined with a declining employment count indicates aggressive policy tightening amid weakening labor demand, which sharply increases discount rates and equity risk premiums, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS/PAYEMS signal fails to predict negative returns in at least 50% of historical instances where rates rise and employment falls concurrently over a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing market downturn, declining housing starts reduce construction-related corporate revenues and household wealth effects, which amplifies the negative impact of elevated interest rates on equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when HOUST declines sharply alongside high FEDFUNDS, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.
